post.description:
This endpoint sends a document to the Dynamic Docs analyzer.
The available Extraction engine is **InvoiceDetails**, which specializes in extracting information from
invoice files and storing it in JSON format.
            
The endpoint responds with a **200 status code** upon the initiation
of the analysis process. Once the analysis is complete, the results can be accessed through the
`GET /insights/results/`.

get.description:
This endpoint retrieves the results of a document analysis previously generated through the `POST /insights/documents/` endpoint.
The **results** field contains the extracted information of the provided document id list, while the other parameters are used to provide
context about the analyzed document.

delete.description:
This operation performs the non-permanent removal of one or more documents within Dynamic Docs.
When executed, all soft-deleted documents are moved to the document retention bin and are removed from
their current locations and projects. If one of the documents is stored in a project, it will be automatically
removed from the project as well.
            
Soft-deleted documents can be restored using the `POST /documents/{id}/restore` endpoint.

patch.description:
This endpoint updates document type, attributes, comments, and hashtags, within Dynamic Docs.
This operation utilizes HTTP PATCH, enabling partial updates of the document's metadata. Only the provided fields are modified, while all others remain unchanged.

delete.description:
This operation allows for the non-permanent removal of a specific document within Dynamic Docs.
When executed, the soft-deleted document is moved to the document retention bin and is removed from
its current locations and projects. If the document is stored in a project, it will be automatically
removed from the project as well.
            
The soft-deleted document can be restored using the `POST /documents/{id}/restore` endpoint.

post.description:
Enables document searching within the Dynamic Docs system and returns the documents that match the search criteria.
            
It combines both the **Match** and **Range** criteria.
            
The **Match** functionality empowers the implementation of precise attribute matching, allowing the application of various filtering conditions within a single request to retrieve matching documents.
In the specific scenario of **locationAttributes**, documents linked to locations possessing matching attributes will also be included in the results.
            
The **Range** functionality of this endpoint is designed to filter data based on date ranges, which can be both absolute and relative.
Absolute dates are specified as "YYYY-MM-DD" or "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss.00000," while relative dates encompass values like "now," "now-365d" (365 days before the current date), "now-55d" (55 days before the current date), "now-12M" (12 months before the current date), and "now-3M" (3 months before the current date).
Date range limits can extend from 0 to 365 days before the current date and 0 to 12 months before the current date.
            
Furthermore, the **Range** functionality can be utilized to filter data based on file sizes. Float numbers can be provided for Greater Than or Equal (Gte) and Less Than or Equal (Lte) conditions.
If an upper bound is not provided, it will match all file sizes greater than the specified one. If a lower bound is not provided, it will match all file sizes smaller than the specified one.

parameters.name: pageNumber
parameters.description: Search result page number.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: Number of records on each page.

parameters.name: allFields
parameters.description: Defines if the endpoint will return all the metadata or only the id and name of the document.

parameters.name: sortBy
parameters.description: Allows custom order of search results based on specific criteria. For multiple values, separate them with commas. Add a '-' before each value for descending order or '+' for ascending order.

post.description:
This endpoint allows you to restore a document in Dynamic Docs that was previously deleted using either
`DELETE /documents` or `DELETE /documents/{id}`. The restored document will
return to its original location, and if it was stored within a project, it will be automatically reinstated
in that project as well.
            
Please note that only document IDs of previously deleted documents can be used with this endpoint.
Attempting to use the IDs of documents that have not been deleted will result in an error.

post.description:
This endpoint processes the document identified by the given document ID using OCR.
            
If the `version_id` query parameter is omitted, the latest version of the document will be OCR processed.
Alternatively, if the `version_id` is provided, the specified document version will undergo OCR.
            
It is crucial to emphasize that document processing will only occur if the MCV OCR module is enabled for the respective tenant.

get.summary: Retrieves OCR text from a Single Document.
get.description:
This endpoint fetches OCR text from a single document in DynamicDocs.
            
If the `version_id` query parameter is omitted, the OCR text from the latest document version is retrieved.
Otherwise, if the `version_id` is provided, the OCR text from the specified document version is returned.
            
Keep in mind that this endpoint only allows Single Document Ocr text retrieval.
If there is more than one `Id`, system will only retrieve the first one specified

patch.summary: Add Or Remove Linked Locations to a Document
patch.description:
This endpoint facilitates the addition or removal of linked locations to a specific document identified by its document ID.
Users can independently perform both add and remove actions simultaneously.
The request parameters include a list of hierarchical locations to be added or removed.
It's important to note that if you attempt to link to a nonexistent location, the system will create it.
            
You Have to keep in mind the following validations:
            
When you want to add a linked location the system will validate the follow:
1) The Content exist
2) The Content Is already located in the location you provide
3) The Content Is already linked to the location you provide
            
When you want to remove a linked location, the system will validate the follow:
1) The Content exist
2) The Content Is linked to the location you provide.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description:
This is the Document ID of the document stored in the final location within Dynamic Docs,
meaning it is not in the staging location.

post.description:
This endpoint allows you to attach documents within an email message for being sent to a designated email address
and be directly added to Dynamic Docs. This feature requires establishing a rule based on the subject of the email
to determine where the documents will be saved.

Files.description: Files to be sent within the email message.

post.description:
This endpoint processes a list of LinkedDocuments, establishing links between documents and locations.
            
A document cannot be linked to a location if:
            
1. The specified document ID does not exist.
2. The document is already located in the given location.
3. The document is already linked to the location.
            
The endpoint returns the ID of the created LinkedDocument entities, along with the associated document ID and location information.

post.description:
Create a hierarchical Location structure where a primary location is established,
and subsequent nested secondary and tertiary locations can be organized within it.

get.description:
This endpoint facilitates the retrieval of locations based on their hierarchical structure, utilizing the parent location's type and identifier.
            
This endpoint is designed to assist in searching for locations that meet specific criteria. It necessitates specifying the parent location's
type, which can be either "PrimaryLocation", "SecondaryLocation" or empty, in conjunction with the parent location's unique identifier.
            
The locations returned by this endpoint are the children locations that align with the specified criteria. For instance, if the parent location type
is set to "PrimaryLocation" and the parent location identifier is set to 1, the endpoint will provide all names and ids of child locations
associated with the PrimaryLocation bearing an ID of 1.

parameters.name: parentLocationType
parameters.description:
This value specifies the parent location type and is restricted to either PrimaryLocation, SecondaryLocation or empty for search purposes.
- When ParentLocationType is empty, it returns all PrimaryLocations within the tenant.
- If ParentLocationType is PrimaryLocation, it fetches the associated SecondaryLocations.
- If ParentLocationType is SecondaryLocation, it retrieves the related TertiaryLocations.

parameters.name: parentLocationId
parameters.description:
It represents the identifier associated with a parent location, which can correspond to either the PrimaryLocation, SecondaryLocation or an empty value.
This value cannot be empty if a non-empty ParentLocationType is used.

parameters.name: quti
parameters.description: Quorum unique tenant identifier

parameters.description: Email of the authenticated user within the tenant, responsible for the API call.

parameters.description: End User Email responsible for the API Call. This value is optional, if is not provided, Authentication User email should be used.

parameters.name: source

if they don't have any linked documents.

post.description:
Creates the list of location attributes for a specific location,
which is determined by the **locationType** and **locationId** parameters.
If any of the specified location attribute names already exists,
this operation will throw a bad request response; otherwise, it will create them.

put.description:
Updates the list of location attributes for a specific location,
which is determined by the **locationType** and **locationId** parameters.
If any of the specified location attribute names already exists,
this operation will update them and delete the old ones; otherwise, it will create them.
If an attribute with the provided name and value already exists,
an error message will be shown.

parameters.description: This value specifies the location type and is restricted to either PrimaryLocation, SecondaryLocation or TertiaryLocation

post.summary: Create a Document Batch Download Request
post.description:
The Batch Download Endpoint enables users to download multiple documents in bulk by submitting a
list of document IDs and a recipient email address.
            
Upon receiving the request, the system triggers a background process that consolidates
all the requested files into one or more zip archives, ensuring that no single zip file exceeds 4 GB in size.
The total number of files processed in a single request is limited to a maximum of 10,000.
Once the zipping process is complete, the system generates pre-signed download links for each zip file, which are then sent to
the provided email address.The recipient can use these pre-signed URLs to download the zip files directly.
This endpoint offers an efficient way to handle large batch document downloads while ensuring that the
files are securely packaged and easily accessible via email.
            
The endpoint’s documentation includes comprehensive details about the request payload and
response format, along with clear examples to guide users in utilizing the endpoint effectively for bulk downloads.
            
```json
{
    "documentIds": [
        10001, 10002, ... , 1000n
     ],
     "recipientEmail": "jhon.doe@foobar.com"
}
```

parameters.description: Quorum unique tenant identifier

parameters.description: Email of the authenticated user within the tenant, responsible for the API call.

parameters.name: end-user-email
parameters.description: End User Email responsible for the API Call. This value is optional, if is not provided, Authentication User email should be used.

parameters.name: source
parameters.description: Represent the source application or client responsible for the API Call

post.description:
This endpoint facilitates the bulk linking or unlinking of locations to multiple documents.
Users can perform both link and unlink actions simultaneously.
The request parameters include a list of document IDs and the locations to be linked or unlinked.
It's important to note that if you attempt to link to a nonexistent location, the system will create it.
            
You Have to keep in mind the following validations:
            
When you want to link a document to another location the system will validate the follow:
1) The Content exist
2) The Content Is already located in the location you provide
3) The Content Is already linked to the location you provide
            
When you want to unlink a document from another location, the system will validate the follow:
1) The Content exist
2) The Content Is linked to the location you provide.
            
The endpoint will response the total ammount of locations linked and unlinked and the errors that would happen while
the process is being done.

post.summary: Perform Optical Character Recognition (OCR) on multiple documents.
post.description:
This endpoint allows for the processing of a large number of documents using Optical Character Recognition (OCR) in a single request.
It is designed to handle up to 2000 documents per request, making it suitable for bulk operations.
            
The OCR process will extract text from the provided documents asynchronously, which can then be used for various purposes such as search and indexing.
            
Please note that the OCR module must be enabled for the tenant to use this endpoint.
            
The request must include a list of document IDs, and optionally, the document version IDs associated with them.
If the document version ID is not provided, the current version of the document will be processed.
            
If there are any errors with the provided documents, a list of errors with the associated document IDs will be returned.
However, the other documents will still be processed.

This endpoint allows for the retrieval of OCR text from a large number of documents in a single request.

Please note that the OCR module must be enabled for the tenant to use this endpoint.

If the document version ID is not provided, the current version of the document will be processed.
            
If there are any errors with the provided documents, a list of errors with the associated document IDs will be returned.
However, the other documents will still be processed.

post.description:
This endpoint facilitates the movement of multiple documents from their current locations to a specified destination location within Dynamic Docs.
Users can specify whether to create the destination hierarchy if it does not already exist.
            
The request body should include the following details:
- **CreateIfNotExists**: A boolean value indicating whether to create the destination hierarchy if it does not exist.
- **Documents**: A list of document IDs to be moved.
- **SourceLocation**: The location hierarchy that the documents are currently in.
- **DestinationLocation**: The location hierarchy that documents are going to be moved.
            
The endpoint performs the following validations:
1. Ensures that the documents exist.
2. Validates the source location. It checks if the contents are located in that location.
3. Validates the destination location. If CreateIfNotExist is false, the request will fail.
4. Checks if the documents are already in the destination location.
            
If the validation passes, the documents are moved to the specified location. If any validation fails, an appropriate error response is returned.
            
Keep in mind that this service will rename the document with a timestamp to ensure there are no duplicated names in the location.
            
Possible responses:
- **200 OK**: The documents were successfully moved.
- **201 Created**: The documents were successfully moved and the destination hierarchy was created.
- **400 Bad Request**: The request is invalid or the validation failed.
- **404 Not Found**: One or more documents or the destination location were not found.

get.summary: Get Export Jobs for a Tenant
get.description:
This endpoint retrieves batch export jobs for a specific tenant (QUTI).
The response includes job details such as batch ID, GUID, status, and processing dates.
Results are paginated using pageNumber (0-based) and pageSize query parameters.
            
Example query: GET /document-batches/export-jobs?pageNumber=0&pageSize=20
            
Note: pageNumber is 0-based (0 = first page, 1 = second page, etc.)

parameters.description: Search result page number.

parameters.name: sortBy
parameters.description: Allows custom order of search results based on specific criteria. For multiple values, separate them with commas. Add a '-' before each value for descending order or '+' for ascending order.

DateRangeObjectDTO.description:
Filter documents based on a range of last modification dates. You can specify a wide range of date values, including:
- Absolute dates, e.g., "2023-01-01" or "2023-01-01 10:10:10.00000"
- Relative dates, e.g., "now," "now-365d" (365 days before the current date), "now-55d" (55 days before the current date), "now-12M" (12 months before the current date), "now-3M" (3 months before the current date).
            
Please note that date limits can range from 0 to 365 days before the current date and 0 to 12 months before the current date.
You can specify values for both the Greater Than or Equal (Gte) and Less Than or Equal (Lte) bounds, where Gte is the lower bound and Lte is the upper bound.
If you do not provide an upper bound, it will match all dates after the specified one. If you do not provide a lower bound, it will match all dates before the specified one.

PrimaryLocationDTO.description: Represents the primary location details, including optional secondary locations.

id.description: Unique identifier of the project, generated automatically by Dynamic Docs.

name.description: The name of the project, defined by the user in Dynamic Docs.

ProjectDTO.description: Represents information about a project, which is a collection of documents.

name.description: Name of the attribute.

values.description: Collection of values associated with the attribute.

ProjectMultipleValuedAttributeDTO.description: Represents a data transfer object for attributes that can have multiple values in a project.

name.description: Name of the attribute.

value.description: Single value associated with the attribute.

ProjectSingleValuedAttributeDTO.description: Represents a data transfer object for attributes that have a single value in a project.

RangeDTO.description: Represents a range criterion used for attribute filtering based on Greater Than or Equal (Gte) and Less Than or Equal (Lte) conditions.

name.description: The name of the resource attribute.

value.description: The value of the resource attribute.

ResourceAttributesDTO.description: Represents an attribute associated with a location or a project, including the attribute's name and its corresponding value.

name.description: The name of the resource attribute.

value.description: The value of the resource attribute.

availableForMultipleValues.description: A flag indicating whether the attribute is available for multiple values.

ResourceAttributesList.description: Represents an attribute associated with a location or a project, including the attribute's name and its corresponding value.

SearchDocumentRequest.description: Represents a request to search for documents with filtering options.

SearchDocumentResponse.description: Represents a response object for document search operations.

description.description: A detailed description enhancing the explanation of the response.

locations.description: List of locations that match the searching criteria

SearchLocationsResponse.description: Represents the response data for a location search operation, providing information about located locations and their details.

name.description: The name of the document's location.

attributes.description: An optional list of attributes associated with the location.

tertiaryLocations.description: List of tertiary locations associated with the secondary location.

SecondaryLocationDTO.description: Represents a secondary location and may include a list of tertiary locations.

name.description: The name of the attribute.

value.description: The value associated with the attribute.

SimplifiedAttributeDTO.description: Represents a simplified attribute consisting of a name and a corresponding value. Please note that this object does not include Length or Type.

name.description: Specifies the document name of the uploaded document. If the provided string already exists in the location, the document will be renamed.

documentType.description: Specifies the document type of the uploaded document. If the provided string does not exist, a new document type will be created.

SingleDocument.description: Represents an individual document for bulk upload.

creationDate.description: The date when the document was originally created.

lastModificationDate.description: The date of the document's most recent modification.

fileSize.description: The size of the document file, measured in megabytes (MB).

source.description: The upload source of the document file.

pageCount.description:
The page count of the document file.
In case the page count is unknown, this value will be -1.

SystemAttributesDTO.description: System-related attributes for the document, providing key information including creation date, last modification date, version, and file size.

secretValue.description: JSON object containing the updated provider-specific secret fields. Serialized to a JSON string and stored in Azure Key Vault. This value is never persisted in the database.

UpdateCredentialRequest.description:
Request payload for updating an existing import/export credential.
Only the Application.Models.Requests.Credentials.UpdateCredentialRequest.SecretValue can be updated; structural fields (Name, Source, OperationType)
are immutable after creation to keep the AKV secret key stable.

name.description: The updated name of the document.

type.description: The updated type or category of the document.

hashtags.description: A list of updated hashtags associated with the document.

attributes.description: A list of updated attributes linked to the document.

comments.description: A list of updated comments related to the document.

UpdateDocumentRequest.description: Represents the request model for updating document metadata in Dynamic Docs.

name.description: The updated name of the document.

type.description: The updated type or category of the document.

hashtags.description: A list of updated hashtags associated with the document.

attributes.description: A list of updated attributes linked to the document.

comments.description: A list of updated comments related to the document.

contentId.description: The unique identifier of the document.

singleValuedAttributes.description: Collection of single-valued attributes for the project.

multipleValuedAttributes.description: Collection of multiple-valued attributes for the project.

UpdateProjectAttributesRequest.description: Represents a request to update the attributes of a project, including both single-valued and multiple-valued attributes.

description.description: A detailed description enhancing the explanation of the response.

singleValuedAttributes.description: Collection of single-valued attributes for the project.

multipleValuedAttributes.description: Collection of multiple-valued attributes for the project.

UpdateProjectAttributesResponse.description: Represents the response for updating project attributes. This response includes details for both single-valued and multiple-valued attributes associated with a project.

documentType.description: Specifies the document type of the uploaded document. If the provided string does not exist, a new document type will be created.

documentAttributes.description: Represents a list of document attributes associated with the document. If the attributes do not exist, they will be automatically created.

UploadDocumentMetadataDTO.description: Represents metadata related to the uploaded document, including its document type, location and associated attributes.

description.description: A detailed description enhancing the explanation of the response.

documentId.description: The unique identifier for the uploaded document.

versionId.description: The version identifier for the uploaded document.

stagedDocumentId.description: Identifier of the document if it is located in the staging location.

UploadSingleDocumentResponse.description:
Represents the response object after uploading a document to Dynamic Docs.
            
Note that only *stagedDocumentId* will be returned if the document is stored in the staging location.
If the document is stored in a final location, both *documentId* and *versionId* will be returned.
